farmer.dashboard.title: Your Farm Dashboard
farmer.dashboard.description: See your farm at a glance. Track active listings, recent sales, market prices, and local weather in one place.

steps.title: Check your stats
steps.content: Look at the top cards to see your total listings, pending orders, and earnings this month.

steps.title: View market prices
steps.content: Scroll down to the Market Prices section to see current prices for your crops in nearby markets.

steps.title: Check the weather
steps.content: The weather widget shows the 3-day forecast for your area. Plan your harvesting and spraying around it.

steps.title: See recent activity
steps.content: The Activity Feed at the bottom shows your latest matches, messages, and listing updates.

steps.title: Weather card
steps.content: The weather card at the top of your dashboard shows the next 3 days of weather with temperature, rain chance, and soil conditions. Below the weather, you'll see advice specific to each crop you've selected.

steps.title: Soil conditions
steps.content: The soil row shows current soil temperature and moisture at 6cm depth. This is especially important for ginger and turmeric growers — soil must be above 25°C for rhizome sprouting.

steps.title: Crop advisories
steps.content: Each of your selected crops gets a one-line advisory. Green ✅ means good conditions. Amber ⚠️ means watch out. Tap '7-day forecast' for full details.

steps.title: No crops selected?
steps.content: If you see 'Select your crops' on the weather card, tap it to go to Settings and choose which crops you grow. This unlocks personalized weather advice.

tips: Tap any stat card to jump to its detail page.
Pull down on the page to refresh all data with the latest prices and weather.
Check the weather card every morning before field work. The soil temperature and moisture readings help you decide the best time to plant, water, or harvest.

faq.question: Why are market prices not showing?
faq.answer: Prices update every morning. If you see no data, check your internet connection and pull down to refresh.

faq.question: Can I change my location for weather?
faq.answer: Yes. Go to More > Account Settings and update your farm location to get accurate weather.

farmer.listings.title: Manage Your Listings
farmer.listings.description: View, edit, and track all your harvest listings. Filter by status to quickly find active, sold, or expired listings.

steps.title: View your listings
steps.content: All your harvest listings appear here sorted by newest first. Each card shows crop name, quantity, price, and status.

steps.title: Filter by status
steps.content: Tap the filter tabs at the top (Active, Sold, Expired, Draft) to narrow down your listings.

steps.title: Edit a listing
steps.content: Tap any listing card to open it. Then tap the Edit button to change price, quantity, or availability dates.

steps.title: Create a new listing
steps.content: Tap the + button at the bottom right to create a new harvest listing.

tips: Keep your listings updated. Buyers trust farmers who maintain accurate quantities and prices.
Mark listings as sold promptly so you stop getting new match requests.

faq.question: Can I reactivate an expired listing?
faq.answer: Yes. Open the expired listing, tap Edit, set new availability dates, and tap Save. It will become active again.

faq.question: How many listings can I have at once?
faq.answer: You can have up to 20 active listings at a time.

farmer.listings.create.title: Create a Harvest Listing
farmer.listings.create.description: List your harvest for sale. Tell buyers what crop you have, how much, your asking price, and when it will be ready.

steps.title: Select your crop
steps.content: Tap the Crop field and pick from the list, or type to search. Choose the exact variety if available.

steps.title: Enter quantity
steps.content: Type how much you have to sell in kilograms. Be accurate so buyers know what to expect.

steps.title: Set your price
steps.content: Enter your asking price per kilogram in LKR. Check the suggested market price shown below the field for guidance.

steps.title: Set availability dates
steps.content: Pick the date range when your harvest will be ready for pickup. Tap Available From and Available Until.

steps.title: Add photos and submit
steps.content: Tap Add Photos to upload pictures of your crop. Then tap Create Listing to publish it.

tips: Add clear photos of your crop. Listings with photos get 3x more buyer interest.
Price competitively. The suggested market price helps you set a fair rate.
Write a short description mentioning farming method (organic, traditional) if relevant.

faq.question: Can I save a listing as draft?
faq.answer: Yes. Tap Save as Draft instead of Create Listing. You can finish and publish it later from the Listings page.

faq.question: What if my crop is not in the list?
faq.answer: Type the crop name manually in the search field. If it does not appear, select Other and type the name.

farmer.matches.title: Your Buyer Matches
farmer.matches.description: GoviHub matches your listings with buyers looking for your crops. Each match shows the buyer's demand details so you can decide whether to accept.

steps.title: View your matches
steps.content: Each match card shows the crop name, match score percentage, and the buyer's demand details — quantity needed, maximum price they'll pay, variety, grade, and needed-by date.

steps.title: Understand the match score
steps.content: The score (0-100%) shows how well the demand fits your listing based on price, quantity, location, and timing. Higher is better.

steps.title: Accept a match
steps.content: Tap Accept to agree to the deal. The status moves from Proposed to Accepted. The buyer's contact details (name, phone, district) are shown on the card.

steps.title: Complete or dismiss
steps.content: Once goods are delivered, tap Complete. If the deal falls through, tap Dismiss to close the match.

tips: Respond to matches quickly. Buyers often choose the first farmer who accepts.
A score above 80% usually means the buyer is very close to your area and price range.
Check the demand details panel on each match to see exactly what the buyer needs before accepting.

faq.question: What are the match statuses?
faq.answer: Proposed (new match), Accepted (deal agreed), Completed (goods delivered), and Dismissed (match closed). Simple and clear.

faq.question: Can I see the buyer's contact info?
faq.answer: Yes. Every match card shows the buyer's name, phone number, and district so you can coordinate directly.

faq.question: What happens after I accept?
faq.answer: The match moves to Accepted status. Call the buyer using the phone number shown on the card to arrange pickup or delivery. Once done, tap Complete.

farmer.diagnosis.title: AI Crop Diagnosis
farmer.diagnosis.description: Upload a photo of a sick or damaged crop and get an instant AI diagnosis with treatment suggestions.

steps.title: Take a clear photo
steps.content: Use your phone camera to take a close-up photo of the affected leaves, fruit, or stem. Make sure there is good lighting.

steps.title: Upload the photo
steps.content: Tap Upload Photo and select the image from your gallery, or tap Camera to take a new one.

steps.title: Wait for results
steps.content: The AI analyzes your photo in a few seconds. You will see the likely disease name and confidence level.

steps.title: Read the treatment plan
steps.content: Scroll down to see recommended treatments, including organic and chemical options with dosage instructions.

tips: Take photos in natural daylight for the most accurate results.
Include both healthy and affected parts in the photo so the AI can compare.
Try multiple photos from different angles if the first result has low confidence.

faq.question: How accurate is the diagnosis?
faq.answer: The AI is trained on common Sri Lankan crop diseases and is about 85% accurate. Always consult a local agriculture officer for serious outbreaks.

faq.question: Does it work offline?
faq.answer: No. You need an internet connection to upload the photo and get results.

farmer.advisory.title: AI Farming Advisor
farmer.advisory.description: Ask any farming question and get answers powered by AI. Get advice on planting, fertilizer, pest control, and more.

steps.title: Type your question
steps.content: Tap the text field at the bottom and type your farming question in English or Sinhala.

steps.title: Send your question
steps.content: Tap the Send button. The AI will process your question and reply in a few seconds.

steps.title: Read the answer
steps.content: The AI gives a detailed answer with practical steps you can follow. It may include product names and dosages.

steps.title: Ask follow-up questions
steps.content: You can ask more questions in the same chat. The AI remembers what you discussed before.

tips: Be specific. Instead of saying 'my crop is sick', say 'my pepper leaves have yellow spots and are drooping'.
Mention your district. The AI gives better advice when it knows your local conditions.

faq.question: What topics can I ask about?
faq.answer: You can ask about crop diseases, fertilizer schedules, pest control, planting times, irrigation, soil health, and harvest handling.

faq.question: Is the advice free?
faq.answer: Yes. The AI advisor is free for all registered farmers.

farmer.marketplace.title: Supply Marketplace
farmer.marketplace.description: Browse and find agricultural supply shops near you. Find fertilizer, seeds, tools, and equipment from verified suppliers.

steps.title: Browse categories
steps.content: Tap a category like Fertilizer, Seeds, Equipment, or Transport to see available products.

steps.title: Search for products
steps.content: Use the search bar at the top to find a specific product by name or brand.

steps.title: View product details
steps.content: Tap a product card to see full details including price, supplier info, and delivery options.

steps.title: Send an inquiry
steps.content: Tap Inquire Now on a product page to send a message to the supplier asking about availability or pricing.

tips: Check supplier ratings and reviews before sending an inquiry.
Use the location filter to find suppliers close to your farm for cheaper delivery.

faq.question: Can I buy directly through the app?
faq.answer: Currently you send inquiries and arrange payment directly with the supplier. In-app payments are coming soon.

faq.question: How do I know if a supplier is trustworthy?
faq.answer: Look for the Verified badge on their profile. Also check their rating and number of completed orders.

farmer.more.title: Settings & More
farmer.more.description: Manage your account, change language, set notification preferences, and log out.

steps.title: Edit your profile
steps.content: Go to Settings to update your name, email, phone number, district, and language. Your email can be changed from the default beta address to your real email.

steps.title: Change language
steps.content: In Settings, select Sinhala, Tamil, or English under Language Preference. The app will reload in your chosen language.

steps.title: Set notifications
steps.content: Toggle notification switches to choose which alerts you receive — match alerts, price updates, weather warnings, push and SMS.

steps.title: Log out
steps.content: Scroll to the bottom and tap Log Out to sign out of your account.

steps.title: My Crops
steps.content: Scroll to the 'My Crops' section to add, change, or remove the crops you're cultivating. Your crop selections control which weather alerts you receive and what advice appears on your forecast.

steps.title: Alert preferences
steps.content: In the 'Weather Alerts' section, choose how many alerts you want — critical only, warnings too, or all alerts including informational ones.

tips: Update your email address in Settings — the default beta email won't receive real notifications.
Keep your phone number updated so buyers can reach you.
Enable price alert notifications to know when market prices change for your crops.

faq.question: Can I change my email?
faq.answer: Yes. Go to Settings and edit the Email field. Enter your real email address and tap Save Changes.

faq.question: Can I delete my account?
faq.answer: Yes. Go to Settings, scroll to the Danger Zone, and tap Deactivate Account. Contact support to fully delete.

faq.question: How do I switch between farmer and buyer roles?
faq.answer: Tap Switch Role at the top of the More page to access your buyer or supplier account if you have one.

buyer.dashboard.title: Your Buyer Dashboard
buyer.dashboard.description: Track your crop demands, view matched farmers, and monitor your purchase stats all in one place.

steps.title: Check demand status
steps.content: The top cards show your active demands, pending matches, and completed purchases this month.

steps.title: View matched farmers
steps.content: The Matches section shows farmers who have crops matching your posted demands.

steps.title: Track purchases
steps.content: Scroll down to see your recent purchase history with quantities, prices, and delivery status.

steps.title: See market trends
steps.content: The price chart shows how crop prices have changed over the past week to help you time your purchases.

tips: Check your dashboard daily during harvest season for the freshest matches.
Tap any stat card to see its full breakdown.

faq.question: Why am I not getting matches?
faq.answer: Make sure your demand has a reasonable price and quantity. Farmers match based on location, price, and availability dates.

faq.question: Can I see farmers outside my district?
faq.answer: Yes. Go to your demand and increase the search radius or remove the location filter.

buyer.demands.title: Manage Your Demands
buyer.demands.description: View, edit, and track all your crop demand postings. See which demands have matches and which need attention.

steps.title: View your demands
steps.content: All your demand postings appear here. Each card shows crop type, quantity needed, your offered price, and status.

steps.title: Filter demands
steps.content: Tap the filter tabs (Active, Fulfilled, Expired) to find specific demands quickly.

steps.title: Edit a demand
steps.content: Tap a demand card, then tap Edit to change quantity, price, or delivery dates.

steps.title: Create a new demand
steps.content: Tap the + button to post a new crop demand.

tips: Update your demands regularly. Farmers prefer buyers with current, realistic requirements.
Close fulfilled demands promptly so you stop receiving new matches.

faq.question: How many demands can I post?
faq.answer: You can have up to 15 active demands at a time.

faq.question: Can I duplicate an old demand?
faq.answer: Yes. Open any past demand and tap Repost. It creates a copy with new dates that you can edit before publishing.

buyer.demands.create.title: Create a Crop Demand
buyer.demands.create.description: Post what crops you need to buy. Tell farmers the type, quantity, your price, and when you need it.

steps.title: Select the crop
steps.content: Tap the Crop field and choose from the list, or type to search for a specific crop variety.

steps.title: Enter quantity needed
steps.content: Type how many kilograms you need. You can set a minimum and maximum range.

steps.title: Set your offered price
steps.content: Enter the price per kilogram you are willing to pay in LKR. Check the market rate shown below for reference.

steps.title: Set delivery dates
steps.content: Pick the date range when you need the crop delivered. Tap Needed From and Needed By.

steps.title: Submit your demand
steps.content: Review all details and tap Post Demand. You will start receiving farmer matches soon.

tips: Offering a fair market price attracts more farmer matches.
Set a wider date range to get more matches. Narrow date ranges limit your options.
Specify your preferred delivery method so farmers know what to expect.

faq.question: How soon will I get matches?
faq.answer: Matches usually appear within 24 hours if farmers in your area have the crop. During peak harvest, matches can come within minutes.

faq.question: Can I set a location preference?
faq.answer: Yes. By default your demand targets your district. You can expand or narrow the search area in the Location field.

buyer.matches.title: Your Farmer Matches
buyer.matches.description: See farmers who have crops matching your demands. Each match shows the farmer's harvest details so you can compare and choose the best offer.

steps.title: View available matches
steps.content: Each match card shows the crop name, match score, and the farmer's harvest details — quantity available, asking price, variety, grade, and availability dates.

steps.title: Compare matches
steps.content: Look at the match score and the harvest details panel to find the best deal. Higher scores mean better fit on price, quantity, and location.

steps.title: Accept a match
steps.content: Tap Accept to agree to the deal. The status moves from Proposed to Accepted. The farmer's contact details (name, phone, district) are shown on the card.

steps.title: Complete or dismiss
steps.content: Once you receive the goods, tap Complete. If the deal falls through, tap Dismiss to close the match.

tips: Accept matches quickly during peak season. Good farmers get snapped up fast.
Check the harvest details panel on each match to see exactly what the farmer is offering — quantity, price, and availability.
Use the farmer's phone number shown on the card to coordinate delivery or pickup.

faq.question: What are the match statuses?
faq.answer: Proposed (new match), Accepted (deal agreed), Completed (goods received), and Dismissed (match closed). Simple and clear.

faq.question: Can I accept multiple matches for one demand?
faq.answer: Yes, if you need crops from several farmers to fill your total quantity. Each accepted match is tracked separately.

faq.question: Can I see the farmer's contact info?
faq.answer: Yes. Every match card shows the farmer's name, phone number, and district so you can coordinate directly.

buyer.marketplace.title: Supplier Marketplace
buyer.marketplace.description: Browse and buy agricultural supplies. Find fertilizer, seeds, tools, and equipment from verified suppliers.

steps.title: Browse categories
steps.content: Tap a category like Fertilizer, Seeds, or Equipment to see available products from suppliers.

steps.title: Search for items
steps.content: Use the search bar to find specific products by name, brand, or type.

steps.title: View product details
steps.content: Tap a product to see price, description, supplier info, and delivery options.

steps.title: Send an inquiry
steps.content: Tap Inquire Now to message the supplier about availability, bulk pricing, or delivery.

tips: Compare prices from multiple suppliers before making a decision.
Look for bulk discount badges on products if you are buying large quantities.

faq.question: Are supplier prices fixed?
faq.answer: Listed prices are starting prices. Many suppliers offer discounts for bulk orders. Send an inquiry to negotiate.

faq.question: Can suppliers deliver to my location?
faq.answer: Delivery options vary by supplier. Check the Delivery section on each product page for details.

buyer.more.title: Settings & More
buyer.more.description: Manage your account, change language, set notification preferences, and log out.

steps.title: Edit your profile
steps.content: Go to Settings to update your business name, email, phone number, district, and language. Change your email from the default beta address to your real email.

steps.title: Change language
steps.content: In Settings, select Sinhala, Tamil, or English under Language Preference.

steps.title: Set notifications
steps.content: Toggle notification switches to choose which alerts you get — match alerts, price changes, weather, push and SMS.

steps.title: Log out
steps.content: Scroll to the bottom and tap Log Out to sign out of your account.

tips: Update your email address in Settings — the default beta email won't receive real notifications.
Keep your business location updated for better local farmer matches.
Enable match notifications so you never miss a new farmer offering your crops.

faq.question: Can I have both a buyer and farmer account?
faq.answer: Yes. Tap Switch Role at the top of the More page to access your other role.

faq.question: How do I change my email?
faq.answer: Go to Settings, edit the Email field with your real email address, and tap Save Changes.

supplier.dashboard.title: Your Supplier Dashboard
supplier.dashboard.description: Monitor your product listings, incoming inquiries, and sales performance at a glance.

steps.title: Check your stats
steps.content: The top cards show your active listings, new inquiries this week, and total sales this month.

steps.title: View recent inquiries
steps.content: The Inquiries section shows the latest messages from farmers and buyers interested in your products.

steps.title: Track your listings
steps.content: See which products are getting the most views and inquiries so you know what is in demand.

steps.title: Review sales summary
steps.content: Scroll down to see your monthly sales chart and top-selling products.

tips: Respond to inquiries within 24 hours to keep your response rate high.
Tap any stat card to go to its detail page.

faq.question: How is the response rate calculated?
faq.answer: It tracks how many inquiries you reply to within 24 hours. A rate above 90% earns you a Fast Responder badge.

faq.question: Can I export my sales data?
faq.answer: Not yet. This feature is coming soon. For now you can view monthly summaries on the dashboard.

supplier.listings.title: Manage Your Listings
supplier.listings.description: View, edit, and manage all your supply listings including fertilizer, seeds, equipment, and transport services.

steps.title: View your listings
steps.content: All your product listings appear here. Each card shows the product name, price, stock status, and category.

steps.title: Filter by category
steps.content: Tap the category tabs (Fertilizer, Seeds, Equipment, Transport, All) to filter your listings.

steps.title: Edit a listing
steps.content: Tap any listing card, then tap Edit to update the price, stock quantity, or description.

steps.title: Create a new listing
steps.content: Tap the + button at the bottom right to add a new product to your shop.

tips: Mark items as Out of Stock instead of deleting them. You can restock and reactivate later.
Add clear product photos. Listings with photos get 5x more inquiries.

faq.question: Is there a limit on how many products I can list?
faq.answer: You can have up to 50 active product listings at a time.

faq.question: Can I list transport services?
faq.answer: Yes. Choose the Transport category when creating a listing. Specify your vehicle type, capacity, and service area.

supplier.listings.create.title: Create a Supply Listing
supplier.listings.create.description: Add a new product or service to your shop. Include details, pricing, and photos so farmers can find what they need.

steps.title: Choose a category
steps.content: Select the product category: Fertilizer, Seeds, Equipment, or Transport.

steps.title: Enter product details
steps.content: Fill in the product name, brand, description, and unit of measurement (kg, litre, piece, etc.).

steps.title: Set the price
steps.content: Enter your price per unit in LKR. You can also add a bulk discount price if you offer one.

steps.title: Add photos
steps.content: Tap Add Photos to upload product images. Show the product clearly from multiple angles.

steps.title: Publish your listing
steps.content: Review everything and tap Publish Listing. Your product will be visible to farmers and buyers immediately.

tips: Include the brand name and size in your product title for easy searching.
Write a clear description mentioning usage instructions and suitable crops.
Set accurate stock quantities so farmers know if the product is available.

faq.question: Can I offer delivery for my products?
faq.answer: Yes. Toggle on Delivery Available and enter your delivery area and fee. Farmers will see this on your listing.

faq.question: How do I update the price later?
faq.answer: Go to your Listings page, tap the product, tap Edit, change the price, and tap Save.

supplier.inquiries.title: Customer Inquiries
supplier.inquiries.description: View and respond to messages from farmers and buyers who are interested in your products.

steps.title: View new inquiries
steps.content: New inquiries appear at the top with a blue dot. Each shows the customer name, product they asked about, and message.

steps.title: Read the full inquiry
steps.content: Tap an inquiry to see the full message, including the quantity they need and their location.

steps.title: Reply to the customer
steps.content: Type your response in the message field and tap Send. Include availability, pricing, and delivery details.

steps.title: Mark as handled
steps.content: After you respond, the inquiry moves to the Responded tab. Mark it as Completed once the deal is done.

tips: Reply quickly. Customers often contact multiple suppliers and go with the first response.
Include your phone number in the reply so the customer can call for urgent orders.

faq.question: Can I receive inquiry notifications on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes. Go to More > Notifications and enable Inquiry Alerts. You will get a push notification for each new inquiry.

faq.question: What if I am out of stock on the requested item?
faq.answer: Reply honestly and suggest an alternative product if you have one. Customers appreciate transparency.

supplier.more.title: Settings & More
supplier.more.description: Manage your supplier account, shop details, language, notifications, and more.

steps.title: Edit shop profile
steps.content: Go to Settings to update your contact name, email, phone number, and district. Change your email from the default beta address to your real email.

steps.title: Change language
steps.content: In Settings, select Sinhala, Tamil, or English under Language Preference.

steps.title: Set notifications
steps.content: Toggle notification switches to control alerts for inquiries, price changes, and system updates.

steps.title: Log out
steps.content: Scroll to the bottom and tap Log Out to sign out.

tips: Update your email address in Settings — the default beta email won't receive real notifications.
Complete your shop profile fully. Shops with accurate contact info get more trust from farmers.
Enable inquiry notifications so you never miss a potential sale.

faq.question: How do I change my email?
faq.answer: Go to Settings, edit the Email field with your real email address, and tap Save Changes.

faq.question: How do I get the Verified Supplier badge?
faq.answer: Complete your profile, upload a business registration document, and maintain a response rate above 80% for 30 days.

auth.login.title: Log In to GoviHub
auth.login.description: Sign in to your GoviHub account to access your dashboard, listings, and matches.

steps.title: Open the login page
steps.content: Tap Log In on the home screen or go to the login page from the menu.

steps.title: Enter your credentials
steps.content: Type your registered phone number or email and your password.

steps.title: Tap Log In
steps.content: Tap the Log In button. If your credentials are correct, you will be taken to your dashboard.

tips: Use Remember Me to stay logged in on your personal device.
If you forgot your password, tap Forgot Password to reset it via SMS or email.

faq.question: I forgot my password. What do I do?
faq.answer: Tap Forgot Password on the login page. Enter your phone number or email and follow the instructions to create a new password.

faq.question: Can I log in with Google?
faq.answer: Yes. Tap Continue with Google to sign in using your Google account.

auth.register.title: Create Your Account
auth.register.description: Register for a free GoviHub account to start buying, selling, or supplying agricultural products.

steps.title: Open registration
steps.content: Tap Create Account on the home screen or the Register link on the login page.

steps.title: Choose your role
steps.content: Select Farmer, Buyer, or Supplier. This sets up your dashboard for your needs. You can add more roles later.

steps.title: Fill in your details
steps.content: Enter your name, phone number (required — 9 to 15 digits), district, and create a password. Your email can be updated later in Settings.

steps.title: Verify your phone
steps.content: You will receive an SMS with a verification code. Enter the code to confirm your phone number.

steps.title: Complete registration
steps.content: Tap Create Account. You will be taken to your new dashboard.

tips: Use a phone number you check regularly. Important notifications go there.
Choose a strong password with at least 8 characters including a number.

faq.question: Is registration free?
faq.answer: Yes. Creating a GoviHub account is completely free for all users.

faq.question: Can I change my role later?
faq.answer: Yes. You can add additional roles (farmer, buyer, supplier) from the More > Switch Role page at any time.

auth.selectCrops.title: Select Your Crops
auth.selectCrops.description: Tell GoviHub which spice crops you grow. This lets us give you weather advice, soil alerts, and farming tips customized for YOUR crops — not generic weather.

steps.title: Tap the crops you grow
steps.content: You'll see cards for all 8 spice crops — black pepper, turmeric, ginger, cinnamon, cloves, nutmeg, cardamom, and mixed spices. Tap any crop to select it. A green border and checkmark will appear.

steps.title: Select 1 to 5 crops
steps.content: Pick the crops you are actively cultivating right now. You can always add or remove crops later from Settings.

steps.title: Tap 'Continue'
steps.content: This saves your selections and takes you to your dashboard, where you'll immediately see weather advice for your crops.

steps.title: Skip for now
steps.content: If you're not sure yet, tap 'Select Later'. You can set your crops anytime from the Settings page. But the sooner you do, the sooner you get personalized weather alerts!

tips: The more accurately you set your crops, the better your weather alerts will be. A cinnamon farmer needs different weather advice than a ginger farmer.
You can change your crop selections anytime from Settings → My Crops.

farmer.weather.title: 7-Day Weather Forecast
farmer.weather.description: Your personalized weather forecast for the next 7 days, with advice specific to the crops you grow. The first 3 days are highly reliable. Days 4-7 are an outlook that may change.

steps.title: Reliable forecast (Days 1-3)
steps.content: The top section marked 'Reliable' shows the next 3 days with detailed weather — temperature, rain chance, wind speed, soil temperature, and soil moisture. These predictions are accurate enough to make planting and harvesting decisions.

steps.title: Read the crop advice
steps.content: Below each day's weather, you'll see advice for each of your selected crops. A green ✅ means conditions are good. An amber ⚠️ means take caution. A red 🚨 means take action immediately.

steps.title: Check soil conditions
steps.content: Each day card shows soil temperature at 6cm depth and soil moisture level. This is critical for crops like ginger and turmeric — they need soil above 25°C for sprouting. Cinnamon needs moderate moisture for bark peeling.

steps.title: View hourly detail
steps.content: Tap 'Hourly' on any day to see the hour-by-hour breakdown — when exactly rain is expected, how soil temperature changes through the day, and what time is best for field work.

steps.title: Extended outlook (Days 4-7)
steps.content: The bottom section marked 'Outlook' shows days 4-7 in a compact format. Use this to plan ahead, but be aware these predictions may change. Check back daily for updates.

tips: Check the forecast every morning before going to the field. Weather can change, and our data updates every hour.
Pay special attention to rain probability above 40% — if you're growing black pepper or ginger, heavy rain can cause waterlogging and root diseases.
The soil temperature reading is especially important if you're planting new ginger or turmeric — don't plant if soil is below 25°C.
If you see a dry spell of more than 7 days predicted, start planning irrigation for your cardamom and ginger.

faq.question: Where does the weather data come from?
faq.answer: We use Open-Meteo, which combines the best weather models from European, American, and Asian weather services. The data is updated every hour with the latest satellite and radar information.

faq.question: Why are days 4-7 less detailed?
faq.answer: Weather predictions become less accurate further into the future. Research shows that tropical weather forecasts for Sri Lanka are most reliable for 3 days. We show 7 days so you can plan ahead, but mark the difference clearly.

faq.question: The forecast was wrong yesterday. Why?
faq.answer: Tropical weather is harder to predict than temperate weather due to sudden monsoon changes. Our forecast is the most accurate available, but no forecast is 100% perfect. The closer to the day, the more accurate it is.

faq.question: I don't see advice for my crops. Why?
faq.answer: You need to select your crops first. Go to Settings → My Crops and add the crops you're growing. Once selected, personalized advice will appear on every forecast day.

farmer.weather.hourly.title: Hourly Weather & Soil Detail
farmer.weather.hourly.description: Hour-by-hour weather and soil conditions for a specific day. Use this to plan exactly when to work in the field — when to water, when to harvest, and when to stay indoors.

steps.title: Soil temperature chart
steps.content: The top chart shows soil temperature at 6cm depth throughout the day. Soil is coolest in the early morning and warmest in the afternoon. For ginger and turmeric, look for temperatures between 25-30°C — that's the sweet spot for growth.

steps.title: Soil moisture chart
steps.content: The second chart shows how wet the soil is throughout the day. Values around 30-35% mean good moisture. Below 20% means the soil is too dry — consider irrigating. Above 40% after rain means check drainage.

steps.title: Hourly data table
steps.content: The table shows every hour of the day with temperature, humidity, rainfall in mm, and cloud cover. Look for the ⚠️ marker — it shows which hours have rain expected.

steps.title: Crop timing notes
steps.content: At the bottom, you'll see timing advice for your crops. For example, if rain is expected at 3pm, the system might advise finishing pepper harvest before noon, or delaying cinnamon bark peeling until the next dry day.

tips: Early morning (6-8am) is usually the best time for field work — cool temperatures and soil moisture from overnight dew.
If rain is expected in the afternoon, plan your drying operations for morning hours.
Watch the humidity column — when humidity stays above 85% for several hours, fungal disease risk increases for pepper and cinnamon.
Soil temperature below 17°C means ginger and turmeric rhizomes won't sprout. Wait for warmer soil before planting.

farmer.alerts.title: Weather Alerts
farmer.alerts.description: GoviHub monitors the weather every hour and sends you alerts when conditions could affect your crops. You'll get alerts about heavy rain, high wind, temperature extremes, dry spells, and soil conditions — all specific to the crops you grow.

steps.title: Understanding alert colors
steps.content: Red 🚨 = Critical — take action NOW. Amber ⚠️ = Warning — prepare and take precaution. Blue = Information — good to know. Green ✅ = Good conditions for your crop.

steps.title: Read the action items
steps.content: Each warning and critical alert includes a 'What to do' section with specific actions. For example, 'Clear drainage channels before heavy rain' or 'Check vine supports before high winds'.

steps.title: Mark alerts as read
steps.content: Tap on any alert card to mark it as read. The blue dot will disappear. You can also tap 'Mark all read' at the top right to clear all unread alerts at once.

steps.title: Check the bell icon
steps.content: The bell icon at the top of every page shows how many unread alerts you have. A red number badge means you have new alerts to check.

tips: Check your alerts every morning — they update throughout the night as new weather data arrives.
Don't ignore warnings. For example, a 'heavy rain + waterlog risk' warning for black pepper means you should clear drainage channels BEFORE the rain arrives, not after.

Alerts are generated based on your specific crops. If you grow both ginger and cinnamon, the same rain event might be a warning for ginger (waterlogging risk) but good news for cinnamon (moisture for growth).
Go to Settings → Alert Preferences to choose whether you want all alerts or only warnings and critical ones.

faq.question: Why am I not getting any alerts?
faq.answer: Make sure you have selected your crops in Settings → My Crops. Alerts are only generated for crops you've selected. Also, if the weather is stable and good, there may genuinely be no alerts to show.

faq.question: Can I get alerts on my phone even when the app is closed?
faq.answer: If you've installed GoviHub as an app on your phone (PWA) and allowed notifications, you'll receive push notifications for critical and warning alerts. This feature is coming soon.

faq.question: The alert says 'heavy rain' but it didn't rain. Why?
faq.answer: Weather alerts are based on forecasts, which are predictions — not guarantees. It's better to prepare for rain that doesn't come than to be caught unprepared by rain that does.

farmer.crops.title: My Crops — Settings
farmer.crops.description: Manage the crops you're currently growing. Your crop selections control which weather alerts you receive and what advice appears on your weather forecast.

steps.title: Add a crop
steps.content: Tap '+ Add Crop' to see the available spice crops. Tap any crop card to add it to your list. It will appear immediately in your My Crops section.

steps.title: Set the growth stage
steps.content: For each crop, use the dropdown to set the current growth stage — Seedling, Vegetative, Flowering, Harvesting, or Dormant. This helps GoviHub give you stage-specific advice. For example, rain during flowering affects black pepper pollination.

steps.title: Enter the area (optional)
steps.content: You can enter how many hectares you have for each crop. This is optional but helps us give better advice in the future.

steps.title: Remove a crop
steps.content: Tap the 'Remove' button on any crop and confirm. You'll stop receiving weather alerts for that crop. You can always add it back later.

steps.title: Update as seasons change
steps.content: Remember to update your growth stages as your crops progress. Change from 'Seedling' to 'Vegetative' when plants establish, to 'Flowering' when flowers appear, and to 'Harvesting' when you begin picking.

tips: Keep your growth stages current — the system gives different advice for flowering pepper (protect from rain) versus vegetative pepper (rain is fine).
If you stop cultivating a crop, remove it from your list so you don't get unnecessary alerts.
You can have up to 5 crops selected at once. If you grow more, select the ones most affected by weather.

faq.question: Do I need to select crops to see the weather?
faq.answer: No — the basic weather forecast (temperature, rain, wind) is always visible on your dashboard. But crop-specific advice and alerts only work after you select your crops.

faq.question: What is the growth stage used for?
faq.answer: Different growth stages have different weather sensitivities. Flowering black pepper is sensitive to rain (it reduces pollination), but vegetative pepper actually benefits from rain. By telling us the stage, we give you the right advice at the right time.

faq.question: I grow rice too. Can I add it?
faq.answer: Currently GoviHub Spices focuses on 8 spice crops only. Rice, vegetables, and other crops may be added in a future version.

common.feedback.title: Submit Feedback
common.feedback.description: Help us improve GoviHub. Report bugs, suggest features, or share your experience with us.

steps.title: Open feedback form
steps.content: Tap the Feedback button in the More page, or tap the Help icon and select Give Feedback.

steps.title: Choose feedback type
steps.content: Select Bug Report if something is broken, Feature Request if you want something new, or General Feedback for other comments.

steps.title: Write your feedback
steps.content: Describe the issue or suggestion clearly. Include what you were doing when the problem happened.

steps.title: Submit
steps.content: Tap Submit Feedback. Your message goes directly to the GoviHub team.

tips: For bug reports, include the steps to reproduce the issue. This helps us fix it faster.
Check the feedback history to see updates on issues you reported previously.

faq.question: Will I get a response to my feedback?
faq.answer: We review all feedback. Bug reports receive a status update. Feature requests are considered for future releases.

faq.question: Can I attach screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. Tap Attach Image in the feedback form to include screenshots showing the issue.

admin.ads.title: Advertisement Management
admin.ads.description: Manage banner advertisements shown on user dashboards. Create ads with images, set targeting by role and district, schedule start/end dates, and track impressions and clicks.

steps.title: View all ads
steps.content: The main page shows all advertisements with their status, impression count, click count, and CTR. Use the filter to show only active or inactive ads.

steps.title: Create a new ad
steps.content: Click '+ New Ad' to open the creation form. Upload a banner image (800×450 recommended, JPEG/PNG/WebP, max 2MB), enter title in English and optionally Sinhala, add a click URL, and set targeting options.

steps.title: Target specific users
steps.content: Choose which user roles (farmer, buyer, supplier) see the ad. Optionally restrict to specific districts. Leave districts empty to show to all users.

steps.title: Schedule ads
steps.content: Set a start date and optional end date. Ads only appear during their scheduled period. Leave end date empty for ads that run indefinitely.

steps.title: Track performance
steps.content: Click 'Stats' on any ad to see detailed analytics: impressions and clicks by day, by user role, and by district. The CTR (click-through rate) shows the percentage of impressions that led to clicks.

steps.title: Pause or delete
steps.content: Click 'Pause' to temporarily hide an ad without deleting it. Click 'Delete' to permanently remove an ad.

tips: Use high-quality 800×450 images for the best display on mobile devices.
Ads appear on farmer, buyer, and supplier dashboards below the weather card.
The Sinhala title and description are shown to users browsing in Sinhala language.
Ads with lower display order numbers appear first.

faq.question: Where do ads appear?
faq.answer: Ads appear as a carousel banner on all user dashboards (farmer, buyer, supplier) and optionally on the landing page. They sit below the weather card.

faq.question: What image size should I use?
faq.answer: Recommended: 800×450 pixels (16:9 aspect ratio). Maximum file size: 2MB. Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP.

faq.question: Can I target ads to specific districts?
faq.answer: Yes. When creating or editing an ad, specify target districts. Leave empty to show to all users regardless of location.
